title: tabernaemontana hoabinhensis
intro: Tabernaemontana hoabinhensis, also known as the Hoabinh tabernaemontana or the Vietnamese rose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in Vietnamese medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. In herbal preparations, Tabernaemontana hoabinhensis is often used as a decoction, made by boiling the plant's roots or stems in water, to create a medicinal tea or infusion.
